name: Yu Da-Hyun
description: The Yu Da-Hyun method is an advanced Megaminx speedsolving technique. It follows the star, F2L, variant Westlund S2L, and last layer structure. Its key innovation is a variant of the Westlund S2L approach where the solver uses a random last layer color rather than a fixed one, allowing for more flexible and sometimes more efficient S2L solutions.

steps.name: Star
steps.description: Solve the first layer edges to form a star on the bottom face of the Megaminx. This is the standard Megaminx opening that provides the foundation for subsequent layers.
tips: Plan the star during inspection for maximum efficiency
Prioritize edges that are already close to their correct positions
A well-planned star leads to easier F2L solutions

steps.name: First 2 Layers (F2L)
steps.description: Solve the first two layers by pairing corner and edge pieces and inserting them into their correct positions around the Megaminx.
tips: Look ahead to future pairs while inserting the current one

steps.name: Variant Westlund S2L (Random LL Color)
steps.description: Solve the second-to-last layer using a variant of the Westlund approach. Instead of committing to a fixed last layer color, the solver selects whichever LL color results in the most efficient S2L solve. This flexibility can significantly reduce move count.
tips: Evaluate multiple potential LL colors before committing
The random color choice is this method's key strength — use it wisely
Practice recognizing which color choice leads to fewer S2L moves
Familiarity with the Westlund S2L approach is a prerequisite

steps.name: Last Layer
steps.description: Solve the last layer using Megaminx OLL and PLL algorithms. The last layer color was chosen during S2L to optimize the overall solve.
tips: The chosen LL color may yield easier or harder LL cases
Practice LL algorithms for all possible top colors
Fast recognition is critical for competitive times

pros: Random LL color choice can significantly reduce S2L move count
More adaptable to scramble than fixed-color methods
Builds on established Westlund S2L knowledge
Competitive at the highest level of Megaminx solving
cons: Evaluating the best LL color adds thinking time
Requires familiarity with LL algorithms for multiple top colors
Higher cognitive load during S2L decision-making
